database_migration_guide.overview: Comprehensive guide for migrating from SQLite to PostgreSQL during the modernization process. This includes schema transformation, data type mapping, and JPA entity creation.

constraint.rule: PostgreSQL MUST use emptyDir volumes only
constraint.rationale: No PersistentVolumeClaims allowed - ephemeral storage acceptable for demo
constraint.implementation: Use emptyDir in Kubernetes Deployment, not StatefulSet

constraint.rule: Schema must be auto-created by Hibernate
constraint.rationale: No manual schema creation - use spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto
constraint.implementation: Set ddl-auto to 'update' or 'create-drop' in application.yml

constraint.rationale: Demo data must be loaded automatically on startup

phase_1_schema_analysis.title: Analyze SQLite Schema
phase_1_schema_analysis.description: Extract and understand the existing SQLite database structure

commands.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';" > tables.txt

relationship.description: One user can have multiple accounts
One account can have multiple transactions

phase_2_type_mapping.title: SQLite to PostgreSQL Type Mapping
phase_2_type_mapping.description: Map SQLite data types to appropriate PostgreSQL types

mapping.notes: Use BIGINT for IDs to support larger datasets
Use VARCHAR with length for constrained fields, TEXT for unlimited
CRITICAL: Use NUMERIC for money, never REAL or DOUBLE PRECISION
For binary data like images or documents
Use proper temporal types instead of text

transformation_example.sqlite_table: CREATE TABLE accounts ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, account_number TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, account_type TEXT NOT NULL, balance REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1, created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) );

phase_3_jpa_entities.title: Create JPA Entity Classes
phase_3_jpa_entities.description: Transform database tables into JPA entities with proper annotations

guideline.note: Optional if class name matches table name

guideline.attributes: - name: column name - nullable: NOT NULL constraint - unique: UNIQUE constraint - length: VARCHAR length - precision/scale: for NUMERIC

guideline.purpose: Store enum as string in database

phase_4_data_migration.title: Migrate Data with data.sql
phase_4_data_migration.description: Create data.sql file to seed the PostgreSQL database with demo data

password_generation.description: Generate BCrypt hashed passwords for data.sql

configuration.application_yml:
spring: sql: init: mode: always # Always execute data.sql data-locations: classpath:data.sql continue-on-error: false # Fail if data.sql has errors
jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: update # Create/update schema automatically defer-datasource-initialization: true # Execute data.sql after schema creation

phase_5_postgresql_configuration.title: PostgreSQL Configuration
phase_5_postgresql_configuration.description: Configure Spring Boot to connect to PostgreSQL
phase_5_postgresql_configuration.application_yml_config:
spring: datasource: url: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL:jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/abcbank} username: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME:abcbank} password: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD:changeme} driver-class-name: org.postgresql.Driver
# HikariCP connection pool settings hikari: maximum-pool-size: 10 minimum-idle: 5 connection-timeout: 30000 idle-timeout: 600000 max-lifetime: 1800000 pool-name: AbcBankHikariPool
jpa: database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect hibernate: ddl-auto: ${SPRING_JPA_HIBERNATE_DDL_AUTO:update} naming: physical-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.PhysicalNamingStrategyStandardImpl implicit-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.ImplicitNamingStrategyJpaCompliantImpl show-sql: ${SPRING_JPA_SHOW_SQL:false} properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect format_sql: true use_sql_comments: true jdbc: batch_size: 20 order_inserts: true order_updates: true generate_statistics: false
sql: init: mode: always data-locations: classpath:data.sql continue-on-error: false
# Defer data.sql execution until after schema creation jpa: defer-datasource-initialization: true

kubernetes_environment_variables.description: Environment variables injected in Kubernetes Deployment
kubernetes_environment_variables.variables: env: - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL value: "jdbc:postgresql://abc-bank-postgres:5432/abcbank" - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: abc-bank-db-secret key: database-user - name: SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: abc-bank-db-secret key: database-password - name: SPRING_JPA_HIBERNATE_DDL_AUTO value: "update" - name: SPRING_JPA_SHOW_SQL value: "false"

causes.cause: Database user lacks CREATE TABLE permission

solutions.solution: Grant CREATE permission to database user
Ensure entities are in scanned package
Check logs for Hibernate schema generation messages
Use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency

causes.cause: Incorrect service name in JDBC URL

solutions.solution: Wait for PostgreSQL pod to be ready
This is expected - emptyDir is ephemeral
data.sql will repopulate on next startup
For production, use PersistentVolumeClaim (not allowed in this demo)

validation_checklist.item: BigDecimal used for all monetary values
